title: Boat Rental Budapest Danube: Prices, Tips & Best Boats 2026
html:
On a stretch of the River Danube that divides Buda from Pest, you can hire a boat at the Vigadó Square pontoons and explore the city from the water for a full-day charter starting around €800. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners offering everything from speedboats to luxury flybridges.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Budapest Danube?

Prices range from €20 for a shared river tour to over €2,000 for a crewed luxury yacht for the day. A private half-day rental of a small motorboat typically costs €250, €500, while a full-day catamaran charter with skipper runs €800, €1,500. Costs vary by vessel size, season, and onboard amenities.

  • Shared sightseeing tour: €20, €40 per person for 1 hour
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from €300
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from €900
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day (up to 8 guests): from €1,800

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Budapest Danube?

Most rental boats under 15 metres can be operated without a licence if you have basic boating experience. The operator confirms exact requirements; for larger vessels or bareboat charters, an ICC or equivalent is typically required. Always check the listing for licence prerequisites before booking.

In summer (June, September), the prevailing breeze is the northwesterly Kósza wind, which can create a gentle chop near Margaret Island. Book a morning slot to avoid afternoon crowds and catch the calmest conditions on the river.

Where to set off in Budapest Danube

Popular departure points include the Vigadó Ferry Terminal, Danube Marina at Római Part, and the boat ramp at Kopaszi-gát. Many listings depart from the quayside at Margaret Island (Margitsziget) or from the southern tip of Óbuda Island. For a more scenic start, consider launching from the quiet marina at Lágymányosi Bay near the National Theatre. Each spot offers easy access to the main navigation channel and views of Parliament, the Chain Bridge, and Gellért Hill.

Which boat to rent

  1. Axopar 28, Ideal for a sporty day out with up to 8 guests, half-day from €350.
  2. Four Winns H350, Comfortable bowrider for a family cruise (max 10 guests), full-day from €500.
  3. Fairline Squadron 49, Spacious flybridge motor yacht for 8 guests with cabins, full-day from €1,200.
  4. Princess V58, Luxurious 58-footer for up to 10 guests, full-day from €1,500; you can browse listings for this motor yacht on BoatHire24.
  5. Lagoon 450, Stable catamaran perfect for a party trip (max 12 guests), full-day from €900.

When is the best season for boating in Budapest Danube?

High season runs from June to September, when temperatures average 25°C and the river is busiest. May and October offer mild weather and lower prices. The Danube’s current is modest, but watch for floating debris after spring rains. A full-day charter covers about 20 nautical miles round trip from Vigadó to the northern bend at Szentendre and back.

At a glance

  • Price band: €20 to €2,500 per person per trip
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 1 hour to 8 hours
  • Max capacity: up to 12 guests (common)
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Budapest Danube

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Budapest Danube?

Yes, many boats can be self‑skippered if you hold a valid licence or prove experience, but the operator sets the rules for each vessel.

What is the minimum age to rent a boat in Budapest Danube?

Typically you must be at least 18 years old to charter a boat, though some operators permit younger drivers with oversight.

Are there any restrictions on where I can take the boat on the Danube?

You must stay within the Budapest city section of the river and observe speed limits near bridges and ferry crossings.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Most boat rentals allow you to bring your own catering, but some premium offerings include a full catering package.

To start planning your trip, hire a boat on BoatHire24 and compare verified listings. If you own a vessel in Budapest Danube, you can list a boat for free and reach thousands of travellers. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Budapest Danube or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Budapest Danube and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
